queue.question: Do large share buyback announcements (>5% market cap, or first-ever program) generate positive abnormal returns in the 3-5d window? Academic literature suggests +2-4%. Event source: 8-K Item 8.01. Advantage: announced any time, not just at earnings.
queue.reasoning: Buyback signal has strong literature support, clear event timing (8-K or press release), and clean causal mechanism (reduced float + management signal of undervaluation). Need to find events via EDGAR Item 8.01 with share repurchase filter for >5% market cap magnitude.
queue.findings: SIGNAL WEAKENED BY LITERATURE. Key findings: (1) Academic consensus: long-horizon abnormal returns on buyback announcements dropped to near-zero post-2007 as markets became efficient to this signal. (2) Short-term (3d) CAR is still ~1.4-1.6% per European studies, but US large-caps show diminishing effect. (3) First-ever buyback announcements are NOT reliably positive -- Salesforce first-ever B buyback (Aug 2022) caused a 10% DROP because investors read it as management signaling low-growth outlook. (4) Large routine buybacks (Apple B, MSFT B, etc.) have muted 1d stock reactions because they are expected and priced in. (5) The sub-signal with most promise: small/mid-cap companies announcing buyback for first time AND buying back >10% of float. Academic paper (Vermaelen) documents 16% long-horizon excess returns for large tender-offer buybacks, but tender offers are rare and well-known. (6) DECISION: Do not backtest this as-is. The causal mechanism (undervaluation signaling) is well-documented but efficiency has eroded the edge. NEXT STEP: If pursuing, narrow to (a) first-ever programs at companies with <B market cap, (b) buying back >10% of shares outstanding, (c) announced with earnings miss (contradicts growth pessimism narrative). This sub-hypothesis has not been tested and may have edge.

queue.question: Compute median abnormal returns (not just mean) for CEO/CFO vs non-CEO/CFO clusters. Winsorize at 99th percentile. Skewness is 2.38 at 5d — mean may be inflated by outliers.
queue.reasoning: High skewness (2.38-3.45) means mean is driven by extreme events. Wilcoxon confirms median is positive (p<0.0001) but median magnitude unknown. This affects position sizing and stop-loss decisions.
queue.findings: CEO/CFO cluster (2bbe0f04) robust stats: mean=+5.90% but heavily right-skewed (skewness=8.23). Winsorized mean=+5.23% (outliers contribute ~0.67%). Median=+1.59%. 10th percentile=-5.05% (stop-loss at 10% covers ~10% of events). Sharpe vs 0 = 0.278. n6_9 (416556df) has better distribution: skewness=1.90, Sharpe=0.370. Mean for CEO/CFO is real but inflated by right tail. Position sizing: K uniform is appropriate; the 90th percentile is +21.58% so the upside tail justifies the position. The median is more representative of typical outcome: expect ~1.5-2% per trade, not 5-7%.

queue.question: Test pre-earnings run-up signal on mid-cap universe (B-B market cap). Large-cap S&P 500 shows dead end (avg +0.23%, fails multiple testing). Use Russell 2000 components or screener. Need at least 50 stocks with 12+ quarters of data (2021-2025). Target: avg abnormal > 0.5%, pos_rate > 50%, p < 0.05 (Wilcoxon).
queue.reasoning: Large-cap version dead-ended due to market efficiency. Mid-cap test is natural follow-up. Frazzini & Lamont (2007) documented drift in 1980s-90s data when market efficiency was lower for smaller stocks.
queue.findings: DEAD END confirmed. Mid-cap (45 stocks, N=407 beater events): 5d avg=+0.50% p=0.007, pos_rate=55%. Statistically significant but below min_abnormal_return_pct=1.5% threshold. Not tradeable after costs. Effect has decayed since Frazzini & Lamont (2007). Dead end for both large-cap and mid-cap.

tasks.findings: VIX 4-tier split CONFIRMED to be meaningful. VIX 20-25 vs 25-30 shows very different signal strength.
